title: Cross-browser UI review: 3-engine matrix with separable baseline approval
problem: The Cloverleaf UI Reviewer (reference-impl/prompts/ui-reviewer.md) runs Playwright exclusively against the chromium engine. This means engine-specific rendering and accessibility-tree differences — present in webkit and firefox, which together represent a material share of production traffic — are never surfaced before a task is merged. Additionally, visual baselines under .cloverleaf/baselines/ are silently overwritten whenever compareVisual encounters a new-baseline or dimension-mismatch result, making baseline changes invisible as a distinct review concern. There is no workflow step that lets a human sign off on baseline drift separately from the underlying code change; both are bundled into the final-gate approval, reducing confidence that visual changes are intentional.
solution: Extend the UI Reviewer to a 3-browser matrix (chromium, webkit, firefox) on all diff-affected routes by: (1) adding a 'browsers' list to reference-impl/config/ui-review.json, defaulting to ["chromium"] for backward compatibility, with the full matrix as the recommended value; (2) updating the UI Reviewer prompt to iterate browsers as an outer loop wrapping the existing viewport × route loops, with per-browser escalation when a required browser binary is absent; (3) adopting a baseline naming scheme (resolved by spike) that adds a browser axis to the existing {slug}-{viewport}.png paths; (4) running axe-core on a single configurable engine (likely chromium, resolved by spike) to avoid false-positive divergence from DOM differences axe is not designed to detect cross-engine, while webkit and firefox contribute to visual-diff coverage only; (5) introducing a baseline-approval gate — a new side-flag 'baselines_pending' on the ui-review run artifact, cleared by an explicit human 'approve-baselines' action before the task may advance to qa — without modifying the Standard task state machine, keeping the change reference-impl-local; (6) capping total browser runs at a configurable limit to control wall-clock cost. Spike findings will refine the browser install strategy, baseline file layout, axe portability decision, baseline-approval mechanism, and cost cap values before implementation begins.
unknowns: What is the disk footprint and install mechanism for webkit and firefox under the existing PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=~/.cache/ms-playwright convention — does 'npx playwright install webkit firefox' suffice on all supported platforms, or do those engines require separate handling or on-demand download at review time?

Which baseline file layout best preserves git-diff readability when adding a browser axis: flat {slug}-{browser}-{viewport}.png, per-browser subdirectories (.cloverleaf/baselines/{browser}/{slug}-{viewport}.png), or per-slug bundles?

Is axe-core genuinely engine-portable across chromium, webkit, and firefox in a Playwright headless context, or do DOM/a11y-tree differences between engines produce false-positive divergence that makes cross-engine axe runs unreliable?

Should the baseline-approval workflow be implemented as a new side-flag on the ui-review run artifact (reference-impl-only change) or as a new state-machine transition in the Standard schema, and what is the impact on existing Standard consumers?

What is the realistic wall-clock cost per task for a 3-browser × N-route × V-viewport matrix using the existing preview-server approach, and what cap value (routes × viewports × browsers) prevents unacceptable run times?
acceptance_criteria: A toy change to a site route causes the UI Reviewer to launch chromium, webkit, and firefox in sequence, producing per-browser baseline PNGs and surfacing any engine-specific visual diffs in the findings.
Axe-core accessibility scanning runs on exactly the configured engine (default: chromium) and the axe viewport list, with no duplicate findings from multi-engine axe runs.
Baseline approval can be granted by a human independently of final-gate task approval — a task cannot advance from ui-review to qa while baselines_pending is true.
Removing one browser from the 'browsers' config list causes that engine's baselines and findings to be skipped without affecting results for remaining browsers.
When a required browser binary is absent, the UI Reviewer returns verdict 'escalate' with a per-browser diagnostic message naming the missing engine and the install command.
The total number of browser × route × viewport combinations is bounded by a configurable cap; combinations beyond the cap are skipped and reported as a warning finding.
All changes are contained within reference-impl (prompt, config, lib); no modifications to the Standard schemas or state-machine JSON are required.
The approved Plan decomposes into discrete implementable tasks targeting reference-impl v0.6.
out_of_scope: CI-level parallelisation of browser runs across workers or containers
Mobile-browser emulation beyond the existing mobile, tablet, and desktop viewport matrix
Live-production testing (review remains preview-server driven, as today)
Modifying the Standard task state-machine to add new states for baseline approval
Cross-browser testing of the Cloverleaf site itself (this RFC targets the UI Reviewer tooling, not a specific site)
